retry_reason: Failure is due to memory allocation or module_get failure; these are not retryable in a standard kernel context. On the existing-object path, kref_get never fails.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for a reset_control object. If an existing reset_control with the given index exists and is sharable (or an exclusive but not acquired request matches), the function increments its kref and returns it. Otherwise, it allocates a new reset_control, initializes its kref to 1 (via kref_init), and returns it. The caller receives an owned reference and must call reset_control_put() (or equivalent kref_put) to release it. The function properly handles errors by cleaning up partial allocations; on success it also takes a module reference and a device reference. The caller must check the return value for ERR_PTR errors.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to get or create a reset_control object, managing its reference count via kref_get (on existing) or kref_init (on new). The only non-refcount logic is minimal allocation and initialization, which is intrinsic to the get-or-create pattern.

check.reason: Both operations are supported in the code: kref_get on existing rstc->refcnt and kref_init on new rstc->refcnt. Access paths from return value rstc to refcount fields are consistent with callee info and type chains match.
